old: The interior situation is not good.
new: The internal situation is not good.
reason: 'Interior situation' is unnatural English; 'internal situation' is the correct phrasing for 内部情况.

old: Your accompaniment means a lot to me.
new: Your company means a lot to me.
reason: 陪伴 means 'company/companionship', not 'accompaniment' (which implies music); 'company' is the natural English equivalent.

old: Thank you for your accompaniment all these years.
new: Thank you for your company all these years.

reason: 配 here means 'to match/go well together'; 'join together' is a mistranslation.

old: This shirt joins together well with this pair of pants.
new: This shirt matches well with these pants.
reason: 配 means 'to match'; 'joins together' is awkward and mistranslated.

old: 凭他的努力，一定能成功。
new: 凭一张照片，警察认出了他。
reason: Sentence_1 already uses 凭他的能力一定能成功 (very similar pattern: 凭 + 他的X + 一定能成功). Sentence_3 is a near-duplicate scenario. Replaced with a different usage of 凭.

old: Based on his efforts, he will certainly succeed.
new: Based on a photo, the police recognized him.

old: He asked the celebrity to sign his name.
new: He asked the celebrity to sign an autograph.
reason: 'Sign his name' is ambiguous (whose name?); '签名' in this context means to get an autograph — 'sign an autograph' is idiomatic.
